assembly_instructions: Read Qwen3-235B-A22B as a tall, sparse MoE factory. Tokens enter at the floor (X=0,Y=0) and climb the cyan residual pillar through 94 transformer blocks (8 are rendered, the rest are implied by the etched gridline). Each block has a GQA attention sublayer on the left (64 query heads sharing 4 KV heads at extreme 16:1 ratio — this is the core of Qwen3's small KV footprint) and a fine-grained MoE FFN on the right (128 routed experts, top-8, NO shared expert — Qwen3 dropped the shared expert). The amber column at X=-3.5 is the GQA KV cache. The orange ring at X=6 is the routed-expert bank with its softmax router. The lavender slab at X=2.5/Y=8 is the dual MODE selector — Qwen3 has hybrid thinking modes, with /think and /no_think system tags routing the SAME weights through different decoding budgets. Above at X=4.5/Y=9.5 sits the Dual-Chunk Attention (DCA) + YaRN module that extends the natively-32K-trained model to 128K context. The translucent slab at X=8 marks the parallelism plan and the 36T-token training run. Click any part for FLOPs, memory, parallelism class, scratch-build pseudocode, the dominant bottleneck, and improvement ideas.

info.summary: 235B-parameter MoE LM from Alibaba's Qwen team (22B activated per token). 94 layers × 4096 hidden dim, GQA at extreme 64-Q / 4-KV (ratio 16) for tiny KV footprint, 128 fine-grained routed experts (top-8) with NO shared expert, QK-Norm + RoPE on attention, and a hybrid /think + /no_think mode that shares weights. 32K native context extended to 128K with Dual-Chunk Attention + YaRN. Pre-trained on 36T tokens across 119 languages.
info.description:
Qwen3-235B-A22B is the flagship of the Qwen3 family. Compared with Qwen2.5 it (i) goes MoE (was dense at the top size), (ii) drops the shared expert (Qwen2.5-MoE had one) — every active param now comes from learned routing, (iii) introduces hybrid thinking modes /think and /no_think over a single set of weights, (iv) uses QK-Norm + RoPE rather than the QKV-bias quirks of earlier Qwens, (v) extends to 128K context with Dual-Chunk Attention overlapping YaRN. The MoE uses 128 fine-grained experts at top-8 (similar density to DeepSeek-V3's 256-top-8 but at smaller total scale) with the auxiliary-loss-free balancing trick. Routing is softmax with a small balance bias.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• 16:1 GQA: brutally small KV cache; 4 KV heads is the most aggressive in the open-MoE class.
• 128 fine-grained experts no-shared: removes the always-on FFN cost; routing must carry all capacity.
• Hybrid /think /no_think: one model serves both fast chat and deep reasoning by system-tag.
• Dual-Chunk Attention + YaRN: cheap context extension from 32K → 128K without re-pretraining.
• 36 T multilingual corpus, 119 languages: positions Qwen3 as the most multilingual open MoE.

facts.label: context length
facts.value: 128 K (trained 32 K, extended via DCA + YaRN)

comparisons.delta: Qwen2.5-72B was a dense decoder with QKV bias, GQA at 8 KV heads, no MoE. Qwen3-235B-A22B replaces the dense FFN with 128-expert top-8 MoE (no shared), drops QKV bias, replaces RoPE-only attention with QK-Norm + RoPE, pushes GQA to 16:1, adds hybrid /think /no_think modes, and extends context 32K → 128K via DCA + YaRN. Same tokeniser (151K).

Qwen2-MoE used a SHARED expert plus 64 routed experts at top-8. Qwen3 keeps the fine-grained pattern but DROPS the shared expert and DOUBLES the routed-expert count to 128 — same total active FFN cost but more capacity. Routing changed from sigmoid + aux-loss to softmax + loss-free balance bias.

Both are auxiliary-loss-free fine-grained MoE. V3 has 256 experts top-8 + 1 shared, MLA attention, 671B total; Qwen3-235B-A22B has 128 experts top-8 + NO shared, GQA(64/4), 235B total. V3 has the bigger total capacity and MLA's compressed cache; Qwen3 has aggressive 16:1 GQA so its KV-per-token is actually competitive and its routing footprint is smaller. Qwen3 adds hybrid thinking modes which V3 lacks (DeepSeek-R1 is a separate model).

Versus closed peers, Qwen3 is the most multilingual open MoE (119 languages) and natively supports hybrid /think + /no_think over shared weights — closed peers typically maintain separate fast and reasoning models. Per-token activated is much smaller (22B vs estimated 100B+), trading per-step quality for cost. KV cache at 16:1 GQA is dramatically smaller than full-MHA peers.

parts.name: Input Tokens (V=151,936, 119 langs)
parts.role: Token-ID stream. Multilingual BPE tokeniser inherited from Qwen2.5 (151,936 vocab).

parts.name: Embedding (V=152K, d=4096)
parts.role: Tied embedding / LM head. ~621M params (~1.24 GiB BF16).

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=4096, 94 layers)
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm residual carry through 94 layers.

parts.name: × 94 Transformer Layers
parts.role: Marker for the 94-layer depth. Only 8 are rendered.

parts.name: Block 1
parts.role: First Transformer block. Pre-norm GQA + MoE FFN.

parts.name: Block 2
parts.role: Block 2.

parts.name: Block 3
parts.role: Block 3. Exploded into sub-components on the right (X=2) and left (X=-1.8).

parts.name: Block 4
parts.role: Block 4.

parts.name: Block 5
parts.role: Block 5.

parts.name: Block 6
parts.role: Block 6.

parts.name: Block 93
parts.role: Block 93. (Layers 7–92 elided.)

parts.name: Block 94 (final)
parts.role: Final block before the LM head.

parts.name: Attention · Pre-RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm. γ ∈ R^d learned by AdamW.

parts.name: GQA · QKV Proj (64 Q / 4 KV — extreme 16:1)
parts.role: QKV projection at the most aggressive GQA ratio in the open-MoE class: 64 query heads share only 4 KV heads. Each head 128-dim. Massive KV-cache win.
parts.memory_footprint: W_QKV: d × (n_h + 2·n_kv) × d_h ≈ 38 M params (~76 MB BF16) per layer
parts.algorithm:
Q = x @ W_Q  # [B,T, 64, 128]
K = x @ W_K  # [B,T, 4,  128]
V = x @ W_V  # [B,T, 4,  128]
Q, K = qk_rmsnorm(Q, K)
Q, K = apply_rope(Q, K, base=1_000_000)

parts.name: QK-Norm (RMSNorm on Q & K)
parts.role: Per-head RMSNorm on Q and K BEFORE the dot product. Replaces Qwen2's QKV-bias trick. Stabilises long-context attention.

parts.name: RoPE (base 1,000,000)
parts.role: Standard rotary embedding with a large base (1M) so angles stay distinct out to 128K context after DCA + YaRN extension.

parts.name: FlashAttention-2 (GQA, with DCA at long ctx)
parts.role: Causal attention. Below 32K = standard FlashAttention. Above 32K = Dual-Chunk Attention splits the sequence into chunks and runs intra-chunk + inter-chunk attention separately, reusing the trained-32K kernel.
parts.algorithm:
if T <= 32K:
    out = flash_attn(Q, K, V, mask=causal)
else:
    chunks = split(seq, 32K)
    intra  = [flash_attn(c, c) for c in chunks]
    inter  = dual_chunk_attn(chunks)  # YaRN-rescaled across chunks
    out    = combine(intra, inter)
improvement_ideas: Ring-attention beyond 256K to shard sequence dim across GPUs.
Switch to native 1M training at next pre-train.

parts.name: Attention · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=4096.

parts.name: GQA KV Cache (4 KV heads only)
parts.role: Per-layer paged KV cache. With only 4 KV heads × 128 dim, the cache is ~16× smaller than full MHA.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 2 × 4 × 128 × FP8 = 1 KB / token / layer; 94 layers × 128K ctx ≈ 12 GiB / sequence
improvement_ideas: INT4 KV quantisation for ~2× extra context.
RadixAttention prefix sharing for shared system prompts.

parts.name: FFN · Pre-RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm before the MoE FFN.

parts.name: Top-8 Softmax Router (loss-free balance)
parts.role: Per-token softmax over 128 experts, top-8. Auxiliary-loss-free balancing via a tiny bias updated at each step.
parts.algorithm:
scores = softmax(x @ W_router) + b_balance
topk_idx, topk_w = topk(scores, 8)
topk_w = topk_w / topk_w.sum()
# b_balance updated each step:
# b -= lr_b * (load_e - mean_load)

parts.name: Routed Experts (128, top-8, NO shared)
parts.role: Per-MoE-layer bank of 128 fine-grained SwiGLU experts (d_ff=1536). No shared expert — Qwen3 dropped it. Every active param comes from learned routing.
improvement_ideas: Group GEMM to fuse all 8 expert calls.
FP4 / INT4 weight-only quantisation for inference.

parts.name: Expert All-to-All
parts.role: NCCL all-to-all dispatch + combine. Dominates wall-clock at MoE FFN time.
parts.algorithm:
grouped = bucket_by_expert(x, topk_idx)
recv    = nccl.all_to_all(grouped)
# expert FFN ↓
out_grp = bucket_by_origin(expert_out)
out     = nccl.all_to_all(out_grp)
improvement_ideas: Overlap dispatch with the previous attention O-proj.
Token grouping by destination rank to coalesce sends.

parts.name: Group GEMM (fused expert FFN)
parts.role: cuBLASLt grouped-GEMM fusing all 8 active experts in one launch.
parts.algorithm:
# per group g of routed tokens:
Y_g = X_g @ W_gate[g]
H_g = silu(Y_g) * (X_g @ W_up[g])
O_g = H_g @ W_down[g]

parts.name: FP8 GEMM (E4M3 fwd / E5M2 bwd)
parts.role: Mixed-precision marker: FP8 forward GEMMs, FP8 backward, BF16 master weights.

parts.name: Dual-Chunk Attention + YaRN (32K → 128K)
parts.role: Inference-time context extension. Splits the >32K sequence into 32K chunks, runs intra-chunk attention with the trained kernel, and runs YaRN-rescaled inter-chunk attention to stitch chunks. No re-pretraining needed.
parts.algorithm:
# YaRN: rescale RoPE freqs by NTK-aware interpolation
rescaled_freqs = yarn(rope_freqs, ratio=128K/32K)
intra = flash_attn(chunk_i, chunk_i, rescaled_freqs_local)
inter = sparse_attn(chunk_i, chunks_<i, rescaled_freqs_global)
improvement_ideas: Train at 64K natively to halve the chunk count.
Adaptive chunk size by query density.

parts.name: Mode: /think (CoT + larger budget)
parts.role: Reasoning mode. Enabled by /think system tag. Same weights, different decoding budget and prompt template.

parts.name: Mode: /no_think
parts.role: Direct-answer mode. Same weights, truncated CoT.

parts.name: LM Head (tied)
parts.role: Tied projection back to 152K vocab.

parts.name: LM Loss (cross-entropy)
parts.role: Pre-training next-token loss + RL post-training rewards.

parts.name: AdamW (β1=0.9, β2=0.95)
parts.role: Single AdamW for all parameters. ZeRO-1 sharding across DP.

parts.name: Post-train: SFT + RLHF + Reasoning-RL
parts.role: Multi-stage post-training: SFT on instruction data, preference RL, then long-CoT reasoning RL on math/code/agent rewards.

parts.name: Parallelism Plan: TP × EP × PP × DP
parts.role: Multi-axis parallelism plan. Typical config: TP=8 (within node), EP across 16 nodes for the 128 experts, PP=8 for the 94 layers, DP for replication.
improvement_ideas: Fuse EP all-to-all with the next attention sublayer prologue.

parts.name: 36 T-token Multilingual Corpus (119 langs)
parts.role: Pre-training data plate: ~36T tokens spanning 119 languages, with synthetic reasoning + code data injected.
